Video.summary: Examine the collision of Europeans, Africans, and Native Americans in the New World.
Video.title: Three Peoples Collide

Video.summary: Most of the inhabitants of the Americas arrived from Asia between 40,000 and 2,000 B.C.
Video.title: The Native Americans

Video.summary: Explore the empires and cultural developments of the Aztecs, Incas, and Maya.
Video.title: Mayas, Aztecs, and Incas

Video.summary: Spain and Portugal shifted trade into the Atlantic, leading to New World expeditions.
Video.title: Europeans and Africans

Video.summary: Learn about later religious iconography and artifacts at Chavin.
Video.title: Chavín and the Rise of Religious Authority

Image.alt: Chavín and the Rise of Religious Authority
Chavín and the Rise of Religious Authority
Chavín and the Rise of Religious Authority

Video.summary: Encounter the Salinar culture, a new settlement pattern showing no ceremonial architecture.
Video.title: Cupisnique to Salinar - Elite Rulers and War

Image.alt: Cupisnique to Salinar - Elite Rulers and War
Cupisnique to Salinar - Elite Rulers and War
Cupisnique to Salinar - Elite Rulers and War

Video.summary: Investigate the Paracas tombs of the 1st millennium B.C. and grasp the significance of mummification.
Video.title: Paracas - Mummies, Shamans, and Severed Heads

Image.alt: Paracas - Mummies, Shamans, and Severed Heads
Paracas - Mummies, Shamans, and Severed Heads
Paracas - Mummies, Shamans, and Severed Heads

Video.summary: Learn about the Nazca's remarkable irrigation system and penetrate the mystery of the "Nazca Lines".
Video.title: The Nazca Lines and Underground Channels

Image.alt: The Nazca Lines and Underground Channels
The Nazca Lines and Underground Channels
The Nazca Lines and Underground Channels

Video.summary: We are finally able to reveal the fascinating truths about ancient North America.
Video.title: The Unknown Story of Ancient North America

Image.alt: The Unknown Story of Ancient North America
The Unknown Story of Ancient North America
The Unknown Story of Ancient North America

Video.summary: DNA evidence points to Asia as the origin of all human migration to North America.
Video.title: The First Human Migrations to the Americas

Image.alt: The First Human Migrations to the Americas
The First Human Migrations to the Americas
The First Human Migrations to the Americas

Video.summary: Explore Clovis, the very first American culture.
Video.title: Clovis Man: America's First Culture

Video.summary: Better understand why some Paleo-Indian groups developed hunting and gathering culture.
Video.title: The Archaic Period: Diversity Begins

Video.summary: Compare common views of Native Americans to the realities of what was.
Video.title: Native America: A Story of Survival

Image.alt: Native America: A Story of Survival
Native America: A Story of Survival
Native America: A Story of Survival

Video.summary: Delve into the "Columbian Exchange" and the creation of "new worlds for all."
Video.title: The Columbian Exchange: New Worlds for All

Image.alt: The Columbian Exchange: New Worlds for All
The Columbian Exchange: New Worlds for All
The Columbian Exchange: New Worlds for All

Video.summary: Examine cultures that existed prior to the Spanish Invasion.
Video.title: The Native South and Southwest in the 1600s

Image.alt: The Native South and Southwest in the 1600s
The Native South and Southwest in the 1600s
The Native South and Southwest in the 1600s

Video.summary: Learn how Native Americans and newcomers forged connections.
Video.title: Werowocomoco and Montaup in the 1600s

Video.summary: Consider the imaginary lives of ordinary people who lived on the other side of history.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Other Side of History: Daily Life in the Ancient World
Video.title: Taking on the Other Side of History

Image.alt: Taking on the Other Side of History
Taking on the Other Side of History
Taking on the Other Side of History

Video.summary: Take a look at the lives of our ancestors, from ancient hominids to Homo erectus to the earliest humans.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Other Side of History: Daily Life in the Ancient World
Video.title: Being Paleolithic

Video.summary: Step into the world's earliest permanent settlement: the river banks in Mesopotamia.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Other Side of History: Daily Life in the Ancient World
Video.title: Living in Mesopotamia

Video.summary: Travel to the world's first Western civilization and explore everyday life during the New Kingdom era.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Other Side of History: Daily Life in the Ancient World
Video.title: Being Egyptian

Video.summary: Professor Garland takes you deep inside the lives of an ordinary Egyptian family.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Other Side of History: Daily Life in the Ancient World
Video.title: Belonging to an Egyptian Family

Video.summary: Meet some Egyptian gods'Hathor, Amun-Re, Osiris'and learn about the myths attached to them.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Other Side of History: Daily Life in the Ancient World
Video.title: Practicing Egyptian Religion

Video.summary: Discover Egyptian beliefs about the afterlife and the journey from this world to the next.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Other Side of History: Daily Life in the Ancient World
Video.title: Being a Dead Egyptian

Video.summary: Take a tour of people in the professions that would been available to you in the village of Deir el-Medina.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Other Side of History: Daily Life in the Ancient World
Video.title: Being an Egyptian Worker
